_comment_chapters: Each chapter is a moment in the story. Order matters — the story follows this sequence, not the location order. Images can be remote URLs or local paths like 'assets/photo.jpg'. Set image to null if there is none.

chapters.title: Year 1: Online study via Zoom
chapters.body: My first year at VinUni began entirely online through Zoom. Like many Cohort 2 students, I spent months seeing classmates only as small faces on a screen. I attended classes every day, yet still felt disconnected and unsure where I belonged.

chapters.title: Year 1: Meeting new friends
chapters.body: Everything changed when we finally moved into the on-campus dormitory in the second semester. During quarantine, we spent most of our time together inside the dorm area, and those quiet days unexpectedly brought us closer. I became part of a friend group called “lâu đài tình ái” [a loving castel], and we filled our evenings with movies, late-night karaoke, and long conversations. Even though I had little academic motivation at the time, these friendships became my first anchor at VinUni.

chapters.title: Year 1: The Year COVID Came to the Dorm
chapters.body: First year, locked down in the dorm. We made the best of it — hallway badminton, midnight corridor conversations, and a lot of instant noodles. Stranger times, but somehow the closest we ever got as a floor.

chapters.title: Year 2: Feeling Lost
chapters.body: Although I still found my major interesting, I began losing motivation and questioning why I was studying and what my future pathway might be.

chapters.title: The Tuesday Study Crew
chapters.body: The CS group claimed this corner every Tuesday at 2pm. Unwritten rule — no one ever took our spot. It became less about studying and more about the ritual of showing up.

chapters.title: Caught in the Rain
chapters.body: A tropical downpour out of nowhere. I sheltered under the overhang for forty minutes with half the campus, creating an impromptu networking session while waiting for the skies to clear.

chapters.title: My Favourite Window Seat
chapters.body: Second floor, east corner. Perfect light, the quiet hum of AC, strong WiFi. I started measuring productivity by how often I could claim that seat.

chapters.title: The Algorithms All-Nighter
chapters.body: Library closes at 11pm — had to pack up and move to the lobby. Survived the midterm regardless. The kind of night you don't remember fondly until a year later.

chapters.title: Six AM, Empty Lanes
chapters.body: Cold water, empty lanes, just the sound of splashing. Best mental reset before a long lecture day. Nobody else thought it was worth it — that was the point.

chapters.title: The Inter-Faculty Final
chapters.body: Engineering beat Business 3–2. I scored the winner with a left-foot curler in the 89th minute and sent the stadium into absolute pandemonium. The highlight of my athletic life on this campus.

chapters.title: A Note to Future Me
chapters.body: Thursday drills at 5pm sharp. Fix the first touch. Keep the team close. Keep showing up — the small Thursdays are what made the big October nights possible.
